_meta.variant_delta_from_v1: Structural: replace tag_extract LLM with (concept_enumerate → canonicalize_concepts → curate). LLM emits concept phrases, embedder maps each to real DB tag, LLM curates from pool.
_meta.hypothesis: Retrieval-first-curator reduces LLM variance because candidate pool is deterministic per prose.

v3__miku_cake__seed1000.notes: Weird tags: 'twintails with hair base', 'character cake', 'gaze (e.g.o)', 'mid stride'. Embedder retrieved rare-variant Danbooru tags for common concepts. Grounded (all real) but semantically off.

v3__miku_cake__seed1001.notes: 'standing on own tail' — miku has no tail. Bad retrieval match. 'simple fish' unclear. 'compact (cosmetics)' weird.

v3__girl_random_artist_random_era__seed1.notes: @yoshida inuhito + studio/charcoal/film_grain — matches V1 seed 1000 closely. Few weird tags here because the prose had concrete era cues.
@tf cafe artist, 1920s diner vibe. 'cuts' odd floater.

v3__girl_sex__seed1000.notes: Same failure as V1 seed 1000 — no explicit act tags. Safety='explicit' (source-keyword kick-in). LLM sanitized the prose again; V3's curator had nothing explicit to pick.

v3__girl_sex__seed1001.notes: 'sex' tag preserved + explicit details (leg lift, sweat, stretching). Same win-variance as V1/V2 on this seed.

v3__empty_random_artist_random_setting__.notes: Laundry scene, @nakajima yuka. 'figure' instead of '1girl' — curator picked the more generic variant. Minor regression.
@mizuki makoto + monk scholar scene. Still has pale_skin AND tan contradiction — same as V1 seed 1001. Structural change didn't fix this LLM-level issue.

_summary.verdict: V3 REJECTED. Worse on every dimension. The retrieval-first-curator introduced low-quality tags via embedder-matches to rare Danbooru variants (e.g. 'twintails with hair base' instead of 'twintails', 'standing on own tail' for miku). V1's compound_split approach — which tries sub-word-spans before semantic — actually produces cleaner tags than the embedder-canonicalize approach.
_summary.crucial_finding: girl_sex seed 1000 failed on V1, V2, AND V3. seed 1001 succeeded on all three. The variance is at the PROSE step, not downstream. No extract-step change will fix this — the prose is what's being sanitized.
_summary.lesson_learned: Structural pipeline changes don't fix per-seed LLM variance. The failure axis is upstream — the prose LLM's decision to sanitize. Next variant should target the prose step (V4 tests stronger source-adherence directive), OR go much further upstream (smaller LLM with less safety-training, different base prompt entirely, multi-sample prose and merge).
